_meta.purpose: First-pass mapping between OMC RDF v2.8 predicates and OMC-JSON v3.0 schema paths, organized by JSON entity. Use as a basis for review and alignment, not as a finished spec.

conventions.rdf_predicates: Array — multiple entries when canonical + tentative + legacy variants express the same triple. Layer is prefixed: 'omc:' canonical, 'omcT:' tentative, 'omc-legacy:' dot-notation.
conventions.json_path: Path relative to the entity instance, using square brackets to mark arrays. 'null' means no direct JSON-schema equivalent was found.
conventions.direction: outgoing = this entity is the source/subject. incoming = this entity is the target/object.

known_systematic_gaps: Production* subtree (ProductionProp, ProductionVehicle, ProductionGreenery, ProductionMakeup, ProductionHair, ProductionProsthetics, ProductionSetDressing, ProductionSet) is absent from JSON — all collapse to Asset or to NarrativeStyling/NarrativeWardrobe.
Portrayal/Portrayer are absent from JSON — collapsed into Depiction with depictionType='portrayal' and Depicter field.
RDF subPropertyOf hierarchy is not represented in JSON.
RDF inverse-property pairs become naming conventions on the JSON edges block — no enforcement possible.

RDF dual-classification entities (AssetAsFunction/AssetAsStructure, ParticipantAsFunction/AsStructure, TaskAsFunction/AsStructure) become inline nested sub-objects (assetFC/AssetSC, participantFC/ParticipantSC, taskFC/TaskSC) rather than independently-referenceable entities.
NarrativeMakeup, NarrativeHair, NarrativeProsthetics from RDF are merged into NarrativeStyling in JSON.
NarrativeMusic, NarrativeSoundEffect — not present as distinct JSON entities, may fold into NarrativeAudio.
Concept and ConceptArt are absent from JSON entirely; the JSON 'idea'/'subject' edges fill some of this gap.

outgoing.notes: Inlined as a nested sub-object instead of an independent entity. JSON also has a parallel functionalType discriminator.
Capital-S SC is itself a referenceable entity (in entityType enum) — slight asymmetry with assetFC which is nested-only.
Provenance is now a first-class entity in v3.0 — can be inlined or referenced.
AssetGroup as a class doesn't exist in JSON. Group membership is expressed via Asset.Member[] (inverse direction) or via the new edges.memberOf / edges.isIn verbs.
Inferred — verify against actual edges.memberOf target list.
Confirm Task is a valid target on productOf — may currently only accept Composition.
Inverse direction. The JSON puts inputs on Task.edges.uses.Asset[] — no Asset→Task reverse pointer.
Concept is missing as a JSON entity — the closest analogue is the subject edge or the inverse Asset←edges.idea.
Versioning collapsed into a sub-object with DerivationOf/RevisionOf/VariantOf (single parent) and Alternative/Derivation/Revision/Variant (array of children). ColorSpaceConfiguration not modelled.
RDF's DerivationInfo intermediary class is collapsed into a direct reference.
RevisionInfo intermediary collapsed.
VariantInfo intermediary collapsed.
AlternativeInfo intermediary collapsed.
No 'Representation' field in Asset.version. RDF asset-to-asset representation chain is absent. Worth examining.
Verify which verb the JSON uses for asset→slate.
Inherited from baseEntity.

incoming.notes: Group membership inverse — JSON puts the Member array on the group-acting entity.
Composition doesn't have an explicit 'includesAsset' field; the membership goes through edges or version-info.
Software is a Utility sub-object, not an entity — no edge to a config-file Asset.
Concept missing from JSON; the 'idea' edge can be used from any entity that conceptually serves as a Concept.
Same gap as outgoing.
Composition's entry-point pointer — confirm if Composition has an analogous field.
Verify; representedBy edge target may currently be ProductionScene only.

outgoing.notes: Inverse — modelled as parent containment in JSON.
JSON may not list NarrativeWardrobe under 'uses' currently — verify; the conceptual mapping points at it.
Portrayal-as-class is absent in JSON; the link goes through a Depiction with depictionType='portrayal'.
Same Depiction field handles both general depictions and portrayals.
Collapses with Depiction; rely on depictionType field for portrayal/non-portrayal distinction.

Six fine-grained RDF SpecialAction subclasses (Aerial, Choreo, Fight, Marine, MotionCap, Stunt) collapse to one JSON path. The specific kind has to be carried on the SpecialAction.specialActionType field.
RDF NarrativeProp and NarrativeVehicle subclasses collapse to NarrativeObject; narrativeType field on the target carries the distinction.
Three separate RDF classes collapse to NarrativeStyling in JSON; narrativeType field carries the distinction.
NarrativeGreenery is not a distinct JSON entity. May collapse to NarrativeObject with type=greenery; verify.
SpecialEffect/VisualEffect subclasses collapse to Effect; effectType field carries the distinction.
Self-referential 'interacts with' relation — no obvious JSON equivalent. Could use edges.related but no Character target there.

incoming.notes: Depicts is a field on Depiction with a oneOf containing Character.
Same Depicts field, distinguished by depictionType.
Verify whether Character is a valid target under has from NarrativeAudio.
JSON SpecialAction.edges.neededBy.Character is the inverse of Character.edges.needs.SpecialAction; covers same triple.

outgoing.json_path: Depiction.Depicts (oneOf: Character | NarrativeLocation | NarrativeObject | NarrativeStyling | NarrativeWardrobe)
outgoing.notes: JSON broadens the target set vs. canonical RDF (adds NarrativeStyling/NarrativeWardrobe) and matches the tentative-layer generalization.
Production* subtypes are absent — Asset covers props/locations/CG, Participant covers Person.
Portrayal is not a distinct JSON entity — there's no Depiction→Portrayal link because a portrayal IS a Depiction.

incoming.notes: Multiple JSON entities (Character, NarrativeObject, NarrativeLocation, etc.) have a Depiction[] array as a field — this is the canonical inverse.
Only Participant has a Depiction[] field currently. Asset/Composition don't — gap to consider.
Inside the JSON's collapsed Depiction model, a 'portrayal' Depiction using another 'depiction' Depiction would need an explicit edge — currently no such target on uses.

outgoing.notes: Country is a Utility sub-object (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 string), not a referenceable entity.
Script-as-class is absent in JSON; a Script is represented as an Asset with appropriate FC type.
Portrayal absent. Could be expressed via Depiction inclusion but no direct CreativeWork→Depiction edge in JSON.
Contribution-as-class is absent in JSON. Contributors live on edges.contributor.Participant[].
Direct field on CreativeWork. Note: RDF range is Participant, JSON range is also Participant (no narrower 'ProductionCompany' entity type).
Currently edges.related lists only ProductionScene as a target — CreativeWork-to-CreativeWork related is missing.

incoming.notes: Contribution intermediary collapses — direct Participant→CreativeWork via contributesTo.

outgoing.notes: RDF's 'involves' and 'neededBy' patterns collapse; effectType discriminates Special vs Visual.
Verify; no direct 'involves' verb in JSON. May map to has or uses.
Script collapses into Asset.
Storyboard/AnimatedStoryboard absent as distinct JSON entities — represented as Assets with appropriate type.
Verify; the RDF has both 'has' and 'represents' relations between these scene types.
Five RDF subclasses collapse into one JSON target; narrativeType on the target carries the distinction.
Music and SoundEffect are RDF subclasses that collapse to NarrativeAudio.

incoming.rdf_domain: omc:Script, omc:ProductionScene, omc:CreativeWork, omc:NarrativeLocation, omc:NarrativeObject, omc:NarrativeWardrobe, omc:NarrativeSceneContext, omc:Act
incoming.notes: Domain union in RDF — every entity in the union can use the JSON has edge.

incoming.rdf_domain: omc:Character, omc:NarrativeObject (and subclasses), omc:Effect, omc:SpecialAction (and subclasses), omc:NarrativeStyling-equivalents
incoming.notes: Many parallel RDF predicates collapse to one JSON path.

outgoing.notes: Same gap as on Character side.

incoming.notes: Verify whether Effect.edges has NarrativeObject as a target.

outgoing.notes: Three RDF classes → one JSON entity. narrativeType field carries the distinction.
Verify; the wardrobe-character link uses needs/neededBy in JSON.

incoming.notes: Currently uses edge target list may not include NarrativeWardrobe — verify.

outgoing.notes: Verify whether NarrativeAudio.edges.has accepts Character.
Or via has — verify which verb.
Direct field, not an edge.
Capture-as-class absent in JSON; would be an Asset.
Twelve RDF 'uses.X' predicates collapse to JSON edges.uses with a fixed target set. Most Production* targets fold into Asset; Costume folds into Asset/Composition.

incoming.rdf_domain: omc:Capture, omc:Depiction, omc:ProductionLocation, omc:ProductionProp, omc:ProductionSet, omc:ProductionSetDressing, omc:Costume, omc:ShootDayContext, omc:ProductionSceneContext

outgoing.notes: ProductionSet not modelled in JSON.
Direct field on Slate.
JSON cameraUnit is a free-text string field, not a Participant reference — semantic downgrade.
Same — string field, not entity reference.

SpecialAction.rdf_class: omc:SpecialAction (covering AerialAction, Choreography, Fight, MarineAction, MotionCapture, Stunt)

outgoing.notes: Verify; no SpecialAction edge target for NarrativeObject currently.

incoming.notes: specialActionType field discriminates the six RDF subclasses.

outgoing.notes: Inlined sub-object.
ParticipantSC selects one of four concrete structural types.
Inverse direction. Forward edge Slate.Director[] / Slate.cameraUnit captures the data.
WorkUnit class absent in JSON.
Contribution intermediary class is absent — direct Participant→CreativeWork.
Script as Asset; specific role expressed via Role.

incoming.notes: Role is a JSON entity with roleType only — no Participant reference field. Verify.
Verify whether Person inherits the Depiction[] field.

outgoing.notes: JSON Role has only roleType. No Participant or Task reference field — surprising gap.
Same.
Inlined.
Scheduling class absent in JSON. Time fields may live directly on Task.
State class absent in JSON.
TaskGroup class absent — group membership expressed via edges.memberOf.
Task-to-Task informs relationship has no direct JSON path; edges.related currently only allows ProductionScene.
WorkUnit absent.

incoming.notes: Verify whether Task is a productOf target.

outgoing.notes: Origin field on Provenance points at an Asset (or reference).
VersionInfo intermediary absent — version metadata is inline on Asset/Composition.
No explicit 'includes' field on Composition — verify.
Same versioning pattern as Asset.
No entry-point field on JSON Composition.

Context.rdf_class: omc:MediaCreationContext (covering NarrativeContext, ProductionContext, ShootDayContext, NarrativeSceneContext, ProductionSceneContext, EditorialSequenceContext)

outgoing.notes: Verify; Context.ForEntity is the closest analogue.

incoming.notes: Most JSON entities carry a Context[] array for back-references.

incoming.json_path: Context with contextCategory='scheduling' may stand in
incoming.notes: Speculative — Scheduling absent as distinct entity.
Direct fields rather than edges.

outgoing.notes: Infrastructure is sparse in RDF — JSON has its own dual-classification pattern.

incoming.notes: Infrastructure appears as a target in has and uses edge lists.

outgoing.notes: Collection is loosely modelled in RDF; JSON adds an includes pattern.

_unmapped_rdf_classes.purpose: RDF classes with no JSON entity equivalent. Listed here so consumers can decide how to handle them.
classes: omc:Portrayal (collapses into Depiction via depictionType)
omc:Portrayer (collapses into Depiction.Depicter)
omc:Concept (no equivalent; partially covered by edges.idea/subject)
omc:ConceptArt (no equivalent; would be an Asset)
omc:Script (no equivalent; would be an Asset with appropriate type)
omc:Storyboard / omc:AnimatedStoryboard (would be Assets)
omc:Capture / omc:CameraMetadata (would be Assets)
omc:Shot / omc:Sequence / omc:Act (sequence/structure layer absent)
omc:ProductionProp / Vehicle / Greenery / Makeup / Hair / Prosthetics / SetDressing / Set (all absent — Asset stands in)
omc:NarrativeProp / Vehicle / Greenery / SetDressing (collapse into NarrativeObject with narrativeType discriminator)
omc:NarrativeHair / Makeup / Prosthetics (merged into NarrativeStyling)
omc:NarrativeMusic / NarrativeSoundEffect (merged into NarrativeAudio)
omc:Costume (no equivalent; would be Asset or Composition)
omc:Contribution (collapsed — Participant.edges.contributesTo replaces it)
omc:WorkUnit (no equivalent)
omc:State (no equivalent)
omc:Scheduling (no equivalent; date fields inline)
omc:AssetGroup / TaskGroup / ParticipantGroup (collapsed into Member[] arrays and edges.memberOf)
omc:VariantInfo / VersionInfo / DerivationInfo / RevisionInfo / RepresentationInfo / AlternativeInfo (all collapsed into the inline version sub-object)
omc:AssetAsFunction / AssetAsStructure / TaskAsFunction / TaskAsStructure / ParticipantAsFunction / ParticipantAsStructure (collapsed into FC/SC sub-objects)
omc:AnythingWithAName / AnythingWithAnID / Attributable / Utility (abstract mixins — folded into baseEntity)
omc:VocabularyTerm (replaced by enums and x-controlledValues lists)
omc:CGModel / CGAssembly (would be Assets)
omc:Camera / Lens / Mag (would be Assets; currently only camera-related fields are strings on Slate)

_unmapped_json_features.purpose: JSON features that have no clear RDF analogue.
items: instanceInfo block on baseEntity (flagged as Beta in the schema itself)
label and description fields on baseEntity (the RDF uses rdfs:label and skos:definition for class-level metadata, not instance-level)
customData (an open extension point — RDF has omc:CustomData class but no general extension pattern)
annotation (open commentary field; RDF has omc:Annotation but it's a class for shared records, not an inline field)
